## Deploy the landing page

NA-AI Landing is a standard Next.js project and can be deployed on Vercel or any platform that supports Next.js.

Recommended platform: Vercel.

Also compatible with Netlify, Render, AWS Amplify, and self-hosted Node.js environments.

<Callout title="Core idea">
  Deploy after you update the product copy, SEO metadata, social images, and site URL.
</Callout>

## Pre-deploy checklist

<PreferAvoid
  preferTitle="Confirm before deploy"
  avoidTitle="Avoid shipping with"
  prefer={[
    'site URL updated',
    'SEO title and description updated',
    'Open Graph image replaced',
    'Twitter image replaced',
    'favicon and manifest updated',
    'production build passes',
  ]}
  avoid={[
    'default product copy',
    'placeholder pricing',
    'missing social images',
    'incorrect canonical URL',
    'broken contact flow expectations',
    'untested mobile layout',
  ]}
/>

## Deploy on Vercel

<Steps>

  <Step>
  ### Push the project to GitHub
```bash title="terminal"
git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial NA-AI Landing setup"
git branch -M main
git remote add origin <your-repository-url>
git push -u origin main
```
</Step>
  <Step>
  ### Import the project in Vercel
Import the GitHub repository and select the Next.js framework preset.
</Step>
  <Step>
  ### Configure build settings
```txt title="vercel"
Install Command: pnpm install
Build Command: pnpm build
Output: .next
```
</Step>
  <Step>
  ### Deploy
Trigger the first production deployment and verify the page loads correctly.
</Step>
  <Step>
  ### Add a custom domain
Add your product domain or subdomain in the Vercel project settings.
</Step>
</Steps>

## Domain strategy

For a premium product or demo, prefer a branded domain.

Examples:

```txt
yourproduct.com
app.yourproduct.com
demo.yourproduct.com
```

For PyColors-owned demos, a branded subdomain such as this is stronger than a raw Vercel deployment URL:

```txt
na-ai.pycolors.io
```

<Callout type="info" title="Perception rule">
  A custom domain makes the product feel intentional. A raw deployment URL often feels like a preview.
</Callout>

## SEO checks

Update:

```txt
src/config/site.ts
```

Update social assets:

```txt
public/seo/og-main.jpg
public/seo/twitter-main.jpg
```

Check canonical URL, Open Graph image, Twitter card image, title, description, sitemap URL, and robots configuration.

## Contact form note

The contact form is frontend-only by default.

Before promising lead capture, connect it to an API route, backend, form provider, CRM endpoint, email service, or server action.

<Callout title="Do not overpromise">
  If the form is not connected yet, make the CTA behavior clear or connect it before launch.
</Callout>
